questions.q: The Attorney General of India is the highest what of the Union?
questions.explanation: The Attorney General is the highest law officer of the Union government.

questions.q: Who appoints the Attorney General of India?
questions.explanation: The Attorney General is appointed by the President of India.

questions.q: The Attorney General serves as the chief legal adviser to the:
questions.explanation: The Attorney General is the chief legal adviser to the Government of India.

questions.q: Who was the first Attorney General of independent India?
questions.explanation: M.C. Setalvad served as the first Attorney General of independent India.

questions.q: The Attorney General is part of which branch of the constitutional framework?
questions.explanation: The Attorney General is a law officer of the executive, advising and representing the government.

questions.q: The Attorney General represents the Union government primarily before which court?
questions.explanation: The Attorney General appears on behalf of the government, chiefly before the Supreme Court of India.

questions.q: The Attorney General holds office during the:
questions.explanation: The Attorney General holds office during the pleasure of the President.

questions.q: The Attorney General must be a person qualified to be appointed as a judge of the:
questions.explanation: The Attorney General must be qualified to be appointed a judge of the Supreme Court.

questions.q: The Attorney General is sometimes referred to as the First Law Officer of the:
questions.explanation: The Attorney General is the First Law Officer of the Union government.

questions.q: The office of the Attorney General is created by the:
questions.explanation: The office of the Attorney General is a constitutional office created by the Constitution of India.

questions.q: Under which Article of the Constitution is the office of the Attorney General provided for?
questions.explanation: Article 76 of the Constitution provides for the office of the Attorney General of India.

questions.q: The Attorney General has the right to speak and take part in the proceedings of which body, without a right to vote?
questions.explanation: The Attorney General may speak and participate in proceedings of both Houses of Parliament but cannot vote.

questions.q: The qualification required for the Attorney General is the same as that for a judge of the Supreme Court, which is set out in which Article?
questions.explanation: Eligibility to be a Supreme Court judge is set out in Article 124, and the same qualification applies to the Attorney General.

questions.q: The remuneration of the Attorney General is determined by whom?
questions.explanation: The Attorney General receives such remuneration as the President may determine.

questions.q: In the performance of duties, the Attorney General has the right of audience in:
questions.explanation: The Attorney General has the right of audience in all courts in the territory of India.

questions.q: The Attorney General gives advice to the Government of India on legal matters referred by the:
questions.explanation: The Attorney General advises the government on legal matters referred to the office by the President.

questions.q: The Attorney General is assisted by other law officers of the Union, including the:
questions.explanation: The Solicitor General and Additional Solicitors General assist the Attorney General as law officers of the Union.

questions.q: Unlike a permanent civil servant, the Attorney General is allowed to:
options: Carry on private legal practice within limits
questions.explanation: The Attorney General is not a full-time government servant and may carry on private legal practice, subject to restrictions.

questions.q: The Constitution does not fix a specific term for the Attorney General; the office instead continues at the:
questions.explanation: No fixed term is prescribed; the Attorney General holds office during the pleasure of the President.

questions.q: The Attorney General enjoys the privileges of a Member of Parliament in relation to:
options: Membership of committees as a voting member
questions.explanation: The Attorney General enjoys parliamentary privileges relating to participation but not the right to vote.

questions.q: The role of the Attorney General is to defend and represent the legal interests of the:
questions.explanation: The Attorney General defends and represents the legal interests of the Union government.

questions.q: The Attorney General appears in cases where the Government of India is a party in which type of proceeding before the Supreme Court?
questions.explanation: The Attorney General represents the government in constitutional and other cases where it is a party.

questions.q: The first Attorney General, M.C. Setalvad, held the office for an unusually long unbroken period of about how many years?
questions.explanation: M.C. Setalvad served as the first Attorney General for about thirteen years, the longest tenure in the office.

questions.q: The Attorney General can accept a brief against the Government of India in which situation?
options: Generally not, as it would conflict with the office's duties
questions.explanation: By the conditions and conventions of the office, the Attorney General does not advise or hold a brief against the Government of India.

questions.q: The Attorney General is barred from defending an accused person in criminal proceedings without the permission of the:
questions.explanation: The Attorney General must obtain the government's permission before defending an accused in criminal proceedings.

questions.q: The duties of the Attorney General are partly defined in the Constitution and partly assigned by which authority?
questions.explanation: The Attorney General performs duties of a legal character assigned by the President, in addition to those under the Constitution.

questions.q: The Attorney General is not a member of the Cabinet, marking the office as distinct from that of a:
questions.explanation: Unlike many other countries, the Attorney General of India is not a member of the Cabinet.

questions.q: Article 88 of the Constitution gives the Attorney General the right to take part in proceedings of Parliament and its:
questions.explanation: Article 88 grants the Attorney General the right to speak in and take part in the proceedings of either House and its committees, without voting.

questions.q: The state-level counterpart to the Attorney General, who advises a state government, is the:
questions.explanation: The Advocate General of a state, under Article 165, is the state-level counterpart to the Attorney General.

questions.q: The Attorney General is to discharge the functions conferred by the Constitution or by law and is therefore best described as a:
questions.explanation: Holding a constitutional office under Article 76, the Attorney General is a constitutional functionary rather than an elected or career official.
